market_rules:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the official closing price for NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) on February 9 is higher than the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

If the final session is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official closing price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution.

If no official closing price is published for that session (for example, due to a trading halt into the close, system issue, delisting, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Yahoo Finance. The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Yahoo Finance after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance, specifically the NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) "Close" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/NVDA/history, published under "Historical Prices."

# Prediction: Nvidia Will Have a Difficult Time Living Up to Investors' Lofty Expectations on Feb. 25
By [Sean Williams] – Feb 16, 2026 at 7:06AM EST
## Key Points
*
Artificial intelligence (AI) is Wall Street's hottest trend, with the stock market's largest publicly traded company, Nvidia, leading the charge.
*
Demand for Nvidia's graphics processing units (GPUs) has been borderline insatiable, translating into superior pricing power and mouthwatering margins.
*
A myriad of factors suggest Nvidia's fiscal 2027 outlook won't satisfy investors.
*

# Should You Buy Nvidia Before Feb. 25? Wall Street is Providing a Nearly Unanimous Answer
By [Danny Vena, CPA] – Feb 13, 2026 at 3:02AM EST
## Key Points
*
The paradigm shift of artificial intelligence (AI) has driven Nvidia's relentless rise over the past three years.
*
The company is the leading provider of graphics processing units (GPUs) that power generative AI.
*
While some investors are skittish, Wall Street is almost universally bullish on the chipmaker.
*

agent_action.reasoning: Nvidia's current price is well above $160, and recent news indicates strong bullish sentiment and positive developments in AI and GPU demand. Despite some concerns about meeting high expectations, the stock is expected to perform well, especially with upcoming earnings. Holding the position aligns with the high probability of the stock closing above $160 by the end of February.
